Lore Item
Slot: SHOULDERS
AC: 5
STR: -10 STA: +15 HP: +55
SV DISEASE: -10 SV POISON: +10
WT: 2.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: ALL except DRU NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

RE: off what?
QuoteReply
# Apr 28 2002 at 11:46 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1594570658261
2 posts
This dropped last night off of Deathfang.
▲ Page top
These are studly
QuoteReply
# Jan 20 2002 at 11:49 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1623025158443
9 posts
I read all the posts here. These shoulders are awesome. I'm 60 rogue on Saryn (look me up, Stubie) and I want these. If you have them, I'm paying good $$.

For a rogue, and many other melee, HP is king. This item gives over +100hp which makes them uba. The only real issue is the -saves, but you just pop in your resist gear for that one fight that you need it for and you're set.

For those that think it's crap, I think you're mitaken. Also, if you're bored some time, parse some data and see what 5, 10 or even 20 AC difference makes in the damage you take... you might be surprised at what little difference it really makes.

Stubie of Saryn
▲ Page top
Great for monks.
QuoteReply
# Dec 31 2001 at 1:23 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591772412171
2 posts
I'd take this over many higher AC shoulder pieces. The +STA and +HP are that good. I already have 1K unbuffed AC, I'm more concerned about lasting longer. The strength will lower my DPS to maybe the equivalent of... a warrior. Which leaves a monk wearing this still outdamaging all but rogues. At 60, this is a 105 HP item. Very handy. Of course, by then hopefully you have better.

54 Monk, 54 Cleric, The Rathe.
▲ Page top
RE: Great for monks.
QuoteReply
# Sep 16 2002 at 7:58 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593965792734
105 posts
Actually at 60 this is 115hp for a monk, and to get better before or at 60... think again, it's not that easy ;)

Only "easy" to get shoulders that are better that I can think of (same or more hp) are the ones from Insanity Crawler in AR (15sta 55hp), Barbed Rubicite from CT and Cords from Chardok quest.

There are others of course, but those are by no means easy to get and you can't farm them.

So, these shoulders are very nice and I have sold a couple for 4 times what they are listed for here ;)

EDIT: And yes indeed, they do drop off Deathfang in the Kennel area of Chardok - PH is "an imperial wardog", the one in the middle of three dogs in the pen.

Edited, Mon Sep 16 08:45:59 2002
▲ Page top
Oops
QuoteReply
# Oct 24 2001 at 10:09 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591762681291
91 posts
Hit enter by accident. What I was going to say is that most monks in their right mind would take the engraved bone pauldrons over this (12ac 3dex 5agi -20 cha, same weight). I know I would. The HP is nice, but AC is king, plus -str isnt great for a monk, although it can be replaced, there arent that many +str items for monk, not to say there are none, but a lot of them go in slots where you might want something else like a haste item. Only really big +str items that come to mind for monk are TBB, gauntlets of potence, and gauntlets of mortality(which i have, another situational item). All three of those give 15 str.
Engraved bone pauldrons arent that expensive either, really. On a twink, of course, it would be nice, or for PR based raids(only one i can think of is trak really)When you reach a level at which AC is more important than HP, which personally id say would be around 30-40 and up, youd want to get rid of them.

Viktheren 54 wiz
Fungku 45 monk
Luclin
▲ Page top
RE: Oops
QuoteReply
# Aug 29 2003 at 4:24 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592250265621
70 posts
Stick to being a wiz Vik ;)
▲ Page top
RE: Oops
QuoteReply
# Mar 02 2002 at 3:49 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591763568178
Sage
74 posts
Im a monk in my right mind. I ditched engraved bone for this sucker in an instant. All the information you have given is purely the stuff that people have told you. AC is not king. Strength dosent mean a horses patoot to monks. Just putting this thing on gives me over 100hp , look up some of the tradables that give +100 hp and you tell me how much they go for.
▲ Page top
On Monks
QuoteReply
# Oct 24 2001 at 10:02 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591762681291
91 posts
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 23 2001 at 11:32 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) GO twinks go twinks go!!!
Whoa there
QuoteReply
# Oct 23 2001 at 6:15 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
These are actually PRETTY nice. -10str is bad yeah, but it is the easiest stat the raise. More poison mobs than disease mobs anyway. Trak for one. +15sta is nice, since STA is pretty hard to raise. -10svd IS bad, it's also hard to raise up but the other benefits on this item are worth getting.
▲ Page top
Kinda pathetic...
QuoteReply
# Oct 23 2001 at 10:25 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592250166807
Scholar
69 posts
Actually, these stink. There are some much better shoulder slots that are much easier to get. Carnal pauldrons, Guardian Pauldrons, BS mantle, Drovlarg mantle... I'd take any of those over this.
The only thing that might make this wearable is if you're desperate to get your STA over 100, so you don't fatigue out during melee, so it might be a tolerable item for rangers or bards who typically have trouble getting to 100 STA.
Otherwise, just give this item to one of your low twinks. They could use the extra hp, maybe.
Chardok is a really rough zone. You don't walk into that place hoping to get an item like this.
▲ Page top
RE: Kinda pathetic...
QuoteReply
# Jul 23 2002 at 12:01 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591756476296
26 posts
Wow, these are not pathetic at all. I would kill for a shoulder item that gave me 145hp. I don't think you understand the real reason why people want this item. Oh and 15sta = 90hp for a 60 warrior and 90 + 55 = 145hp. Wow...
▲ Page top
RE: Kinda pathetic...
QuoteReply
# Oct 23 2001 at 11:28 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591753880975
139 posts
Stamina=hp. HP are god. This thing adds over 100 hp(like 140 at 60 for a war). That is awesome, alot of wars I know will be after these.
They drool over those barbed dragonhide ones from kael. Never everyone has the best in every slot, is nice to see another nice HP shoulder item.

Scholar
582 posts
I guess this is more of Verant's idea of "situational equipment" - items that are good for particular circumstances but not the best "all the time". +10 PR would be nice to have against certain Mobs.... Trakanon comes to mind.
